commit.message:
feat(hooks): add pre-commit gitignore guard

Detects staged files that should never be committed (logs, runtime data,
secrets, large files, compiled artefacts, etc.) and automatically:
  1. Adds the specific path to .gitignore (if not already covered)
  2. Unstages the file
  3. Aborts the commit with a clear remediation message

Pattern categories blocked:
  - Log files (*.log, *.log.N)
  - Environment/secrets files (.env, .env.*, secrets.json, config/*.key/pem)
  - Private keys and certificates (*.key, *.pem, *.p12, id_rsa, etc.)
  - Runtime data (data/*.json, data/*.csv, data/*.db)
  - Compiled Python artefacts (__pycache__, *.pyc, *.pyo)
  - Temporary files (*.tmp, *.bak, *.swp)
  - Backup files (backups/, *.backup)
  - Database files (*.db, *.sqlite, *.sqlite3)
  - Node.js dependencies (node_modules/)
  - Python virtual environments (.venv/, venv/, etc.)
  - Large files (>= 2 MB staged blob)
  - Well-known blocked paths (backend.log, config/secrets.json, etc.)

Updated install-hooks.sh to run the guard first in the composite
pre-commit hook (before secret-scan and TODO checks).

Re-run scripts/install-hooks.sh to activate.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🗃️ fix: restrict Onion-Location header to HTML responses only 🗃️

Previously onionLocationMiddleware set the Onion-Location header on
ALL clearnet responses — including SSE streams, JSON API, static
assets, RSS/Atom feeds, and plain-text. This caused two bugs:
1. Tor Browser would abort active SSE search streams when it received
Onion-Location mid-stream and tried to navigate to the .onion URL
2. Non-Tor users saw ERR_CONNECTION_ABORTED because the .onion hostname
doesn't resolve outside of Tor
Fix: Per the Tor Project spec, Onion-Location must only appear on HTML
page responses. Implemented with onionLocationWriter — a ResponseWriter
wrapper that defers setting the header until WriteHeader() is called,
at which point the Content-Type is known. Header is only set when
Content-Type starts with "text/html".
Additional fast-path skips (no wrapping overhead) for:
- /api/* and /static/* and /admin/api/* paths
- Accept: text/event-stream requests (SSE)
- Non-HTML Accept headers (application/json, text/plain, etc.)
- Requests already on .onion (no redirect needed)
onionLocationWriter also implements http.Flusher via Unwrap() so SSE
handlers that reach the writer can still flush normally.
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: system hardening pass — warnings/errors from system check

- fix(gitignore): resolve merge conflict markers; clean up tracked files
  - Removed backend.log, backend/backend.log, frontend/frontend.log from tracking
  - Removed data/settings.json, data/ui_settings.json from tracking
  - Removed test_movement.py from tracking
  - uv.lock kept tracked for reproducible dependency pinning
  - CHAT_MODE_MODEL_SELECTION.md and other stale planning docs now ignored

- fix(health): sensor_health/overall_status rollup always returned 'unknown'
  - Python 3.11 str(StrEnum.MEMBER) returns 'ClassName.MEMBER' not the value
  - Fixed _sensor_status_to_payload() to use .value instead of str()

- fix(tls): TLS probe failed due to PermissionError on letsencrypt dir
  - setfacl grants pi user r-x on /etc/letsencrypt/live/ and archive/
  - Deploy hook at /etc/letsencrypt/renewal-hooks/deploy/fix-permissions.sh
  - Reverted openssl s_client workaround; direct file access is now primary path
  - PermissionError guards now log warnings instead of silently failing

- fix(imu): calibration_status always reported 'unknown'
  - Added _CALIBRATION_LEVEL_MAP (0=uncalibrated..3=fully_calibrated)
  - BNO085 RVC mode limitation documented; reports 'uncalibrated'/'rvc_active'
  - Fixed 'or unknown' fallback in sensor_manager to 'or uncalibrated'

- fix(tof): VL53L0X 8190mm sentinel values leaked into telemetry
  - Renamed _TOF_OUT_OF_RANGE_MM=8000 to TOF_SENSOR_MAX_VALID_MM=8190
  - Sentinel values >=8190 filtered to None in driver and sensor_manager
  - ToFData.distance_mm changed to Optional[int]=None
  - fail_count now increments (not resets) on sentinel reads

- fix(content-length): ASGI RuntimeError: Response content longer than Content-Length
  - SanitizationMiddleware returned exhausted body_iterator on large/invalid-JSON
    fallback path; reconstructed Response(content=raw) to recompute Content-Length
  - JSONResponse(status_code=304) replaced with Response(304) — JSONResponse
    renders None as b'null' (4 bytes) but Starlette omits Content-Length on 304s

- fix(navigation): nav position mismatch after GPS health recovery
  - GPS health 'unknown' caused read_gps() to return None, forcing dead reckoning
  - Added mismatch detection + logging in _update_position on GPS re-acquisition
  - position_mismatch_warn_threshold_m=5.0 logs warning when DR drifted >5m

- fix(rc-local): rc-local.service Exec format error
  - Service now active (exited) with status=0/SUCCESS

commit.message:
feat: pipeline architecture for cover control (v0.7.0) (#59) * refactor: replace monolithic CoverController with pipeline architecture Replaces the ad-hoc flag-soup in apply_calculation with a Laravel-style chain-of-responsibility pipeline. Each pipe handles one concern: EnabledPipe → SleepProtectionPipe → ExitPausedCheckPipe → NoSunPipe → ExitDetectionPipe → PositionDrivePipe → TiltPipe Key changes: - New cover_control/context.py: CoverTrackingState (exit_paused, in_no_sun) persisted per cover via GroupState.cover_states; CoverContext per-cycle - New cover_control/pipes/: one module per pipe - CoverController.apply_calculation now runs Pipeline([...]).run(ctx) - GroupState: removes sun_has_hit_facade, is_first_facade_hit, no_sun_action_applied, obstacle_was_blocking; adds cover_states dict - coordinator/base.py: removes flag-management block from _calculate_slat_angle - __init__.py: removes is_no_sun gate and flag resets from apply_cover_tilts - Sleep protection now checked BEFORE position drive (correct ordering) - Sunrise bypass: NoSunPipe sets first_sun_hit on in_no_sun->sun_active transition; ExitDetectionPipe skips for that one cycle - Reflection protection: detected via calculation.sun_is_behind_facade in NoSunPipe instead of the removed sun_has_hit_facade flag - ARCHITECTURE.md: updated directory tree and added Mermaid pipeline diagram - Tests updated to match new API and pipeline semantics Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * docs: fix Mermaid newlines in pipeline diagram Replace \n with
in node labels for correct rendering. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * docs: clarify threshold in pipeline diagram Label now reads 'manual_open_threshold' instead of the ambiguous 'threshold'. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * refactor(visualize): run charts through real pipeline instead of math-only Previously the visualization script called calculate_slat_angle() directly and simulated sleep/exit/obstacle logic with ad-hoc Python closures. Bugs in the pipeline pipes were invisible in the diagrams. Now every chart runs through the complete cover control pipeline via a lightweight MockHass that immediately updates simulated cover state when service calls are made (so _wait_for_position exits on the first iteration with no real asyncio.sleep). simulate_day() runs all 960 time steps through the real pipe chain: EnabledPipe → SleepProtectionPipe → ExitPausedCheckPipe → NoSunPipe → ExitDetectionPipe → PositionDrivePipe → TiltPipe User actions (sleep, exit) are injected via force_at {minute: (pos, tilt)}. Notable behavioral difference now correctly shown in exit.svg: exit_paused stays True until the next no-sun period (sunset), not until the user lowers the cover — matching the actual pipeline semantics. Also updates pyproject.toml script/* per-file-ignores to allow patterns that are acceptable in utility scripts (D, PTH, C408, ARG). Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(exit-mode): auto-resume tracking when cover is lowered below threshold ExitPausedCheckPipe now checks the current cover position on each cycle while exit_paused=True. If the position has dropped back below manual_open_threshold the user has lowered the cover again, so exit_paused is cleared and tracking resumes immediately without waiting for sunset. Updates exit.svg to show user raising cover at 11:30, pausing until 12:30 when the cover is lowered, and tracking resuming from that point. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): show tilt at 100% during exit mode period When a cover is raised to EXIT_POS the slats physically open to 100%. Set tilt=100 in force_at alongside the position so the chart reflects this reality, matching the old visualization behaviour. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): fix sleep chart to show correct position behaviour Start cover at position=100% (raised overnight, correct) with tilt=0% (user closed slats before sleeping). During sleep SleepProtectionPipe blocks everything so position stays at 100%. When user opens slats at 10:00 the pipeline drives cover down to 0% first then applies the calculated tilt — matching the intended behaviour. Also use no_sun_behavior=keep_last so the no-sun period doesn't fight with the initial state, and add initial_position/initial_tilt params to simulate_day() for flexibility. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): cover position during sleep is 0% (cover driven down) The sleep scenario is: cover was driven to drive_position=0% by prior tracking, user then closed slats (tilt→0%) for blackout. SleepProtection keeps everything frozen at position=0%, tilt=0%. When user wakes, the pipeline resumes tracking (already at position, just re-tilts). Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): raise cover to 100% after sunset in sleep chart Use no_sun_behavior=open (the default). SleepProtectionPipe blocks NoSunPipe before sunrise (tilt=0%), so the overnight position stays at 0%. After sunset the user is awake and tilts are tracking, so SleepProtectionPipe passes and NoSunPipe raises the cover to 100%. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): tilt becomes 100% when cover is raised to 100% position Physical reality: raising a venetian blind fully (position=100%) opens the slats to 100% tilt. MockHass now mirrors this in set_cover_position so the sleep and other charts correctly show tilt=100% after sunset. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * fix(visualize): annotation text boxes float above lines with white bg Add ANNOT_BBOX (white rounded box, alpha=0.85) and zorder=5 to all annotate() calls so labels are never broken by tilt/position lines. Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * feat(visualize): show threshold crossing triggers sleep-protection resume - Add force_at steps at 9:59 and 15:00 where tilt crosses just above the manual_close_threshold (5%), visually showing the exact mechanism - Add orange annotations pointing at the threshold line at both wake moments: 'User raises tilt above 5% → pipeline unlocks & drives cover' - Add siesta-end equivalent annotation at 15:00 Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> * chore: bump version to 0.7.0 Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com> --------- Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Security hardening: capture engine fixes and viewer response sanitization (#3880)

Capture:
- Fix crash when corruptSavePcap re-dispatches packets that already
  exceeded tunnelDepth limit — reset tunnelDepth before CORRUPT
  ethernet callback so mProtocol gets set (fixes NULL deref)
- Fix js0n bounds check: callers pass sizeof(out) in bytes but js0n
  used it as element count, making limit 4x too generous (ALW)
- Fix wise.c domain string corruption: restore colon before two
  early-return paths in wise_lookup_domain
- Right-size TLS JA3 stack buffers based on protocol constraints
  (ja3 30000→13000, eja3 10000→13000, ecja3 10000→13000, ecfja3 1000→1024)
- Prevent symlink attacks in writer-simple: unlink before open with
  O_EXCL to avoid TOCTOU races on pcap output files
- Enlarge AWS credential buffers from 100 to 256 in S3/SQS readers
- Use dynamic allocation for S3 escape and continuation token URIs
  to prevent truncation with long tokens
- Improve signal handler safety: set exit handler first in controlc
  and terminate so double-signal always terminates

Viewer:
- Extend prototype pollution middleware to check req.body
- Set Content-Type text/plain on error responses across CSV, session,
  connection, cyberchef, bodyhash, spigraph, and tag endpoints to
  prevent browsers from interpreting error text as HTML
- Prevent mass-assignment in user layout update endpoints by
  whitelisting allowed fields from req.body
- Stricter regex validation for history index parameter
- Fix hunt role access check: !arr.length was truthy for empty arrays

Tests:
- Add coverage for text/plain error responses and updated endpoints

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
🗃️ fix: healthz no-reload refresh + intent filter toy exception 🗃️

healthz: replace location.reload() with fetch+DOM update
- Full page reload every 30s was causing HTTP/2 GOAWAY frames that
aborted SSE connections on other tabs (search results stream)
- On Tor Browser the reload also triggered Onion-Location redirect
to .onion, failing for users without a Tor proxy configured
- New approach: fetch('/healthz.json', {cache:'no-store'}) on each
30s cycle; applyUpdate() patches status banner, checks table,
stats, and timestamp in-place — no navigation, no connection abort

fix: normalise path separators in all transfer ID-discovery paths; cancel orphaned retries; remove dead filter_by_search_type

Round-8 adversarial review findings:

1. MEDIUM (download.py): All three ID-discovery blocks (initial,
   fallback, late) compared slskd filenames against enqueued_by_fn
   using direct string equality. user_browse constructs forward-slash
   paths but slskd can echo backslash paths — causing zero transfers
   to be tracked and immediate download failure. Fix: build a
   norm_to_fn lookup (fn.replace('\','/').lower() -> canonical fn)
   at enqueue time; apply normalised fallback in all three discovery
   blocks. Consistent with _cancel_transfers_for_filenames and
   _find_new_transfer_id which already normalised.

2. LOW (download.py): When re-enqueue succeeded but
   _find_new_transfer_id returned None, the newly created slskd
   transfer was silently orphaned — consuming bandwidth with no speed
   floor enforcement for the rest of the session. Fix: call
   _cancel_transfers_for_filenames for the untracked filename,
   skipping IDs already in active or existing_ids.

3. LOW (filter.py): filter_by_search_type was dead code — defined
   publicly but never called anywhere. The identical threshold logic
   lived in pick_best_directory._valid_for_type. Removed the
   duplicate function; _SINGLE_MAX_TRACKS/_ALBUM_MIN_TRACKS constants
   remain for _valid_for_type.

fix: guard per-file state machine on poll failure; consistent str() ID coercion

Round-7 adversarial review found two bugs:

1. HIGH: Per-file state machine ran even when poll_failed=True.
   current_by_id is empty on a failed poll, so every active transfer
   appeared as 'vanished' and burned retry budget on a transient API
   blip. With max_retries=3 and multiple files, 3 intermittent poll
   failures would silently abort all downloads. All other timeout/check
   sections already had 'if not poll_failed:' guards — add the same
   guard around the per-file for-loop.

2. MEDIUM: Transfer ID type inconsistency — active dict accumulated a
   mix of raw API values (lines 344, 570) and explicit str() values
   (lines 366, 545). current_by_id was built with raw keys (line 406),
   so current_by_id.get(str_id) could silently return None, triggering
   spurious 'vanished transfer' retries. Fix: str()-ify current_by_id
   keys at build time and all active[fn] = tid assignment sites.
